nodes.title: Anima LLLite Inpainting Guide
widgets_values:
## 5. Inpainting / Anima LLLite

- `PreviewBridge`: Edit the mask and pass it to the inpaint path. Release workflows do not store temporary preview ids.
- `lllite_name`: Recommended: `anima-lllite-inpainting-v1.safetensors`.
- `strength`: Anima LLLite strength. Start from `1.0`; lower it if the effect is too strong.
- `start_percent` / `end_percent`: LLLite application range. Recommended: `0` / `1`.
- `denoise`: Masked-area regeneration strength. `1.0` is strong regeneration; lower it when you need preservation.
- `mod_w_profile`: Mod Guidance upstream default is `step_i8_skip27`. If drift appears, compare with the safer `step_i14`.
- `seed`: Reproduction baseline for inpaint results.

Note: This section cannot run without the `ComfyUI-Anima-LLLite` custom node and the controlnet model. MaskEditor/PreviewBridge can show console warnings or 400/404 errors depending on the ComfyUI frontend version. If that happens, edit the mask again and rerun.

nodes.title: LoRA Section Guide
widgets_values:
## LoRA

- `lora_stack`: LoRAs that will actually be applied. To reproduce the preset style, match the shared preset filenames.
- `strength`: LoRA influence. Higher values strengthen the style and reduce the base model expression.
- `trigger_words`: Passed to Prompt Studio as Trigger Words. Check the preset first before editing prompts manually.
- `style_prompt`: Used by Prompt Studio to assemble style/artist tags.
- `FIX`: Repairs missing LoRA paths by filename when paths differ in a shared workflow.

Note: If a LoRA file is missing or its path differs, failure can happen at the LoRA application step rather than model loading.

nodes.title: T2I Section Guide
widgets_values:
## T2I

- `width` / `height`: Base generation resolution. First run the release defaults, then adjust.
- `Quality Tags`: When Mod Guidance is enabled, these are separated from the positive prompt and sent to the guidance input. Keep the release defaults first, then tune only if needed.
- `use_anima_mod_guidance`: Separates quality tags for ANIMA guidance. When enabled, it uses the `Anima Mod Guidance` model patch. If color or composition becomes too fixed, compare with OFF.
- `mod_w_profile`: Original default is `step_i8_skip27`. Upstream describes it as the best overall quality profile. If a LoRA shows anatomy drift, use the safer `step_i14`. `uniform_w3` is a legacy recovery mode and is not recommended.
- Prompt field `enabled`: Disabled fields are not included in the final prompt.
- Prompt field order: fields are assembled from top to bottom.

Note: Large resolutions, long prompts, and Mod Guidance increase memory use and runtime. If an error occurs, first test with the default resolution and Mod Guidance OFF.

nodes.title: I2I Section Guide
widgets_values:
## I2I

- `LoadImage`: Replace `example.png` with the actual input image.
- `width` / `height`: Used as the reference when converting the input image to latent.
- `megapixels`: Automatic resize limit. Higher values can preserve more detail but increase VRAM use.
- `vae_name`: Used for input image encoding/decoding. Match it with the VAE in the model-load section.

Note: Even if you use only T2I, the I2I path will fail immediately when enabled if LoadImage is not set correctly.

nodes.title: Model Load Guide
widgets_values:
## 2. Model Load

- `unet_name`, `clip_name`, `vae_name`: Core ANIMA model files. Use the filenames provided in the release workflow first.
- `steps_total` / `refiner_step`: Total sampling length and late-stage refinement start. Keep the defaults first.
- `cfg`: Prompt adherence. Start near `8`; too high can break forms.
- `sampler_name` / `scheduler`: Recommended: `euler` / `simple`, or the section default.
- `shift`: Qwen/Flow timestep correction. Keep the default first.

### Anima DAVE Upstream Defaults

- `strength`: Upstream default `0.30`. For a lighter effect, compare from `0.20`.
- `tau`: Upstream default/recommended value `0.10`.
- `mask`: Upstream default mask `dave_alpha.npz`.

### KJNodes Optimization Upstream Defaults

- `TorchCompileModelAdvanced`: `backend=inductor`, `mode=default`, `fullgraph=False`, `dynamic=auto`, `compile_transformer_blocks_only=True`, `dynamo_cache_size_limit=64`, `disable_dynamic_vram=False`.
- `PathchSageAttentionKJ`: default is `sage_attention=disabled`, `allow_compile=False`. Enable it only when the SageAttention library is installed.
- SageAttention install: run `python -m pip install sageattention` with the same Python used by ComfyUI, then restart ComfyUI. For Windows portable, run the same command with embedded Python. `sageattn3` options may require a separately supported GPU/package.
- `enable_fp16_accumulation`: upstream default is OFF. It can error if the installed PyTorch/CUDA stack does not support it.

Note: `torch compile`, `fullgraph`, `dynamic`, `SageAttention`, `fp16 accumulation`, and `disable_dynamic_vram` depend on GPU, CUDA, PyTorch, and SageAttention installation state. If install/import errors occur, disable SageAttention/compile/fp16 options and verify the base path first.

nodes.title: ANIMA Generation Guide
widgets_values:
## 3. ANIMA Generation

### ComfyUI-DCW Upstream Defaults / Starting Points

- `dcw_enabled`: upstream default ON.
- `lambda_l`: upstream default `0.05`. Tooltip starting range is DDPM/EDM `0.04-0.07`; Flux/Flow is about 2x.
- `lambda_h`: upstream default `0.01`. Tooltip starting range is DDPM/EDM `0.008-0.015`; Flux/Flow is about 2x.
- `cwm_enabled`: upstream default ON.
- `alpha_l` / `alpha_h`: upstream defaults `0.0` / `0.0`. When tuning CWM, suggested starts are `alpha_l 0.1-0.3`, `alpha_h 0.1-0.2`. Too much can over-sharpen or over-constrain composition.
- `smc_preset`: upstream default `Off`. `Auto` detects the model family and applies paper defaults. `Custom` is the only mode that uses `smc_lambda` / `smc_k`.
- `smc_lambda` / `smc_k`: upstream defaults `6.0` / `0.1`. The upstream tooltip recommends lambda in `2-8`.

### DiT Spectrum Patch Upstream Defaults / Tuning

- `enabled`: upstream default ON.
- `window_size`: upstream default `2.0`.
- `flex_window`: upstream default `0.25`. Increase for speed, decrease for quality.
- `warmup_steps`: upstream default `6`. Increasing this is the upstream quality-first tuning tip.
- `tail_actual_steps`: upstream default `3`.
- `blend_w`: upstream default `0.3`. Aggressive acceleration example: `flex_window=1.0`, `blend_w=0.7`.
- `cheby_degree` / `ridge_lambda`: upstream defaults `3` / `0.1`.
- `history_size`: upstream default `100`.

Note: DCW, CWM, SMC, and Spectrum depend on custom-node versions and GPU environment. If a failure occurs, isolate in this order: `Spectrum OFF -> SMC Off -> CWM OFF -> DCW OFF`.

nodes.title: HighRes Guide
widgets_values:
## 4. HighRes

- `switch`: Enables HighRes. Recommended: turn it ON after confirming base generation.
- `denoise`: Regeneration strength. Start low; higher values can change composition.
- `scale_by`: Resolution multiplier. Increase gradually according to VRAM.
- DCW / CWM / SMC / Spectrum details follow the `3. ANIMA Generation` guide.

Note: HighRes is one of the most VRAM-heavy sections. If an error occurs, lower `scale_by` and retry with Spectrum/DCW disabled.

nodes.title: Face Detailer Guide
widgets_values:
## 6.1 Face Detailer

- `switch`: Enables the face detailer. Recommended: turn it ON after confirming base generation.
- `SAM3 threshold`: Face detection sensitivity. Keep the default first; lower it for missed faces and raise it for false positives.
- `refine_iterations`: Mask refinement iterations. Keep the default first.
- `SEGS_crop_factor`: Face crop range. Recommended: `4`.
- `bbox_fill`: Bounding-box fill. Recommended: OFF.
- `drop_size`: Removes small detections. Recommended: `100`.
- `contour_fill`: Contour fill. Recommended: ON.
- `guide_size` / `max_size`: Recommended `1024` / `2048`.
- `denoise`: Face regeneration strength. Recommended: `0.33`.
- `feather`: Edge softness. Recommended: `5`.
- DCW / CWM / SMC / Spectrum details follow the `3. ANIMA Generation` guide.

Note: SAM3, Impact Pack, and DCW/Spectrum options depend on installed versions and VRAM. If execution fails, check in this order: keep preview disabled, Spectrum OFF, DCW OFF.

nodes.title: Eye Detailer Guide
widgets_values:
## 6.2 Eye Detailer

- Same structure as the face detailer, but it handles a narrower eye region.
- `SAM3 threshold`: Eye detection sensitivity. Keep the default first.
- `SEGS_crop_factor`: Crop range around the eyes. Recommended: `6`.
- `bbox_fill`: Recommended OFF.
- `drop_size`: Removes small false detections. Recommended: `40`.
- `contour_fill`: Recommended ON.
- `guide_size` / `max_size`: Recommended `1024` / `2048`.
- `denoise`: Eye regeneration strength. Recommended: `0.29`.
- `feather`: Recommended `6`.
- DCW / CWM / SMC / Spectrum details follow the `3. ANIMA Generation` guide.

Note: This runs after 6.1. For error isolation, enable the face detailer and eye detailer separately.

nodes.title: Upscale Guide
widgets_values:
## 7. Upscale

- `switch`: Enables upscaling. Recommended: turn it ON after confirming the save path.
- `upscale model`: Recommended: `2x-AnimeSharpV4_Fast_RCAN_PU.safetensors`.
- `upscale_by`: Recommended: `2`.
- `steps` / `cfg`: Recommended `15` / `8`.
- `sampler` / `scheduler`: Recommended `euler` / `sgm_uniform`.
- `denoise`: Recommended `0.11`.
- `mode_type`: Recommended `Chess`.
- `tile_width` / `tile_height`: Recommended `512` / `512`.
- `mask_blur`: Recommended `8`.
- `tile_padding`: Recommended `128`. Lower it if VRAM is insufficient.
- DCW / CWM / SMC / Spectrum details follow the `3. ANIMA Generation` guide.

Note: Missing upscale model, large resolution, and high `tile_padding` are common causes of errors or VRAM exhaustion. If an error occurs, switch this section OFF or lower tile size/padding.

nodes.title: Image Save Guide
widgets_values:
## 8. Image Save

- `filename_prefix`: Save filename rule. You can combine `%time`, model name, style name, and other fields.
- `output folder`: Result save location.
- `format`: Release default is webp. Switch to png when compatibility matters.
- `quality` / `compression`: Affects webp or png output quality and file size.
- `metadata`: Writes prompt, model, and LoRA information into the image.
- Preview nodes are for checking the final image before saving.

Note: Saving can fail due to folder permissions, invalid filename characters, or image-format support.

nodes.title: Metadata Guide
widgets_values:
## 8.1 Metadata

- `Civitai Hash Fetcher`: Writes Civitai model identification and workflow name into metadata.
- Civitai registration rule: keep `model_name` fixed as `Anima All in One workflow`, and manage only `version` as `v6.0_en`.
- `metadata_prompt` / `metadata_negative_prompt`: Final prompt text stored in the image metadata.
- `LoRA Stack to String`: Converts the used LoRA list into a string.
- `sampler` / `scheduler`: Records sampling information needed for reproduction.
- `GetImageSize`: Includes final image size in metadata.

Note: Civitai/hash-related nodes can depend on model file access and extension versions. If saving is the goal, separate errors in this section from image-save errors.

nodes.title: Workflow Overview
widgets_values:
## Workflow Overview

Read the guide node at the top of each section first, then enable only the parts you need.

1. Prepare the style LoRA preset and trigger/style prompt in `LoRA`.
2. Use either `T2I` or `I2I` as the input path.
3. `2. Model Load` and `3. ANIMA Generation` are the base generation path.
4. Enable `HighRes`, `Inpainting`, `Face/Eye Detailer`, and `Upscale` only when needed.
5. Save the final image and reproduction metadata in `Image Save` and `Metadata`.

### Error-prone Areas

- Optimization options: `torch compile`, `SageAttention`, `fp16 accumulation`
- Custom-node dependent sections: `DCW`, `Spectrum`, `SAM3`, `Impact Pack`, `Anima LLLite`
- Session-state dependent sections: `PreviewBridge`, mask editor, temporary preview images

If an error occurs, first disable the section switch or environment-dependent option and confirm that the base path runs.

nodes.title: Required Models / Download Links
widgets_values:
## Required Models / Download Links

Place the following files in the matching ComfyUI model folders for the sections you use.

### Core ANIMA

- [qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors?download=true)
- [anima-base-v1.0.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/anima-base-v1.0.safetensors)
- [qwen_image_vae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors?download=true)

### Optional / Section Models

- Utility LoRA: [Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B-base-distilled-LoRA.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/hanzogak/Anima-Comradeship/resolve/main/LoRA/Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B-base-distilled-LoRA.safetensors?download=true)
- Inpainting: [anima-lllite-inpainting-v1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/kohya-ss/Anima-LLLite/resolve/main/anima-lllite-inpainting-v1.safetensors)
- Upscale: [2x-AnimeSharpV4_Fast_RCAN_PU.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kim2091/2x-AnimeSharpV4/resolve/main/2x-AnimeSharpV4_Fast_RCAN_PU.safetensors?download=true)

### Custom Nodes That May Need Manual Install

If ComfyUI Manager / Registry automatic installation misses a dependency, install the repository manually and restart ComfyUI.

- [ComfyUI-Anima-DAVE](https://github.com/sorryhyun/ComfyUI-Anima-DAVE)
- [ComfyUI-Anima-LLLite](https://github.com/kohya-ss/ComfyUI-Anima-LLLite)
- [ComfyUI-DCW](https://github.com/namemechan/ComfyUI-DCW)
- [ComfyUI-Spectrum-KSampler](https://github.com/sorryhyun/ComfyUI-Spectrum-KSampler)
- [ComfyUI-Spectrum-sdxl](https://github.com/ruwwww/ComfyUI-Spectrum-sdxl)

### Storage Location

```text
📂 ComfyUI/
└── 📂 models/
    ├── 📂 text_encoders/
    │   └── qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors
    ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
    │   └── anima-base-v1.0.safetensors
    ├── 📂 vae/
    │   └── qwen_image_vae.safetensors
    ├── 📂 loras/
    │   └── Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B-base-distilled-LoRA.safetensors
    ├── 📂 controlnet/
    │   └── anima-lllite-inpainting-v1.safetensors
    └── 📂 upscale_models/
        └── 2x-AnimeSharpV4_Fast_RCAN_PU.safetensors
```

nodes.title: Preset LoRA / Civitai Links
widgets_values:
## Preset LoRA / Civitai Links

These LoRAs are not required core models. They are included to reproduce the style of the release preset.

### LoRA Manager Download Guide

1. Open `LoRA Manager` in ComfyUI and search Civitai, or open the links below.
2. You may use any preferred `loras` folder as the download location.
3. `Anima LoRA Preset` `FIX` can repair paths only when the filename matches.
4. After downloading, run scan/refresh metadata in LoRA Manager.

### LoRA List

- [anima_ch9nb1n_v2.1-epoch36](https://civitai.com/models/2653156?modelVersionId=3006073) - `anima_ch9nb1n_v2.1-epoch36.safetensors` (N0VA39 / v2.1)
- [anima_ci10ranko_v2.0-epoch36](https://civitai.com/models/2647381?modelVersionId=3025710) - `anima_ci10ranko_v2.0-epoch36.safetensors` (N0VA39 / v2.0)
